body_txt: Not every student will be a certified genius and obsess over anything that isn't a flawless A+ . Many will actually do quite poorly in school. A Book Dumb character is a fairly common way to incite relatability with the audience (especially when creating a This Loser Is You ). Some characters are like this due to a lack of formal education—either because of family or community situations—or being from areas without an education infrastructure. They might not be stupid or lazy in any way; a lack of opportunity has simply restricted their progression. It is also possible for them to be an Apathetic Student who simply doesn't want to learn. If they are asked to write an essay, they might end up Padding the Paper . They could also end up being Held Back in School or be constantly on the verge having it happen. Such characters aren't necessarily students. It is also possible for them to be adults, who might be portrayed as having not done well in school. May overlap with Unskilled, but Strong , when a character who lacks formal training makes up for it with raw power. The inversion is Book Smart . Contrast TV Genius , who only seems intelligent in the classroom, and Ditzy Genius , who is a genius in academics but inept in everyday life. Also contrast Insufferable Genius , who is very talented and arrogantly proud of it. Compare Everybody Hates Mathematics , the roughly mathematical equivalent. Compare Street Smart for those who are good at social skills and the most likely form of intelligence to overlap with this trope. Technical opposite of the Badass Bookworm .
